ceipalNote: CEIPAL API does not support name/phone-based filtering on getApplicantsList endpoint — returns full 270K records regardless of search params. Candidate matching was not possible via API. Manual CEIPAL lookups recommended for key candidates.
benchmarkNote: Tet Hernandez and Sheila Marie Alarte are the performance benchmarks per Mike. All other recruiters are measured against their call type patterns. Transcript char count used as duration proxy (~1200 chars/min).

screen.note: Tet's screens range 2K-15.6K chars; excluding Larry pt1 (connection issues), avg is ~9K chars (~7.5 min)

vm.note: Sheila's VM included specific interview details + reference requirement — substantive, not template

followUp.note: Small sample — Tet's follow-up was a brief decline call

interviewPrep.note: NO actual interview prep calls occurred on March 12. Mike's standard: 5-10 min minimum, closer to 10. This is itself a flag.

scoredCalls.summary: Sheila left a voicemail for Kevin Macias informing him that Victor wants to move forward with an in-person interview tomorrow, available from 3:30 PM onwards. She proactively asked about reference availability before the interview confirmation, addressing a known pain point from a previous candidate who fell through on references.
strengths: Proactively addressed reference requirement upfront
Clear message with specific timing
Action-oriented — didn't just leave a vague 'call me back'
improvements: Could have left her callback number in the voicemail

scoredCalls.callTypeNote: Interview scheduling/logistics — NOT actual interview prep (no coaching, no behavioral prep, no company culture discussion)
scoredCalls.summary: Live follow-up with Kevin about the in-person interview scheduling. Kevin pushed back on the short notice (next day). Sheila handled it professionally — acknowledged it's not ideal but explained the competitive situation and urgency without being pushy. Pivoted to asking about references (needs 3, Kevin has 2). Good balance of urgency and empathy. NOTE: This was interview SCHEDULING, not interview PREP — no coaching on what to expect, behavioral questions, or company culture was provided.
flags: No interview prep provided despite in-person interview pending — candidate going in unprepped
strengths: Excellent objection handling — empathetic but firm on urgency
Used competitive framing naturally ('at the end of the day, it's all about competition')
Didn't pressure candidate — offered to ask for alternative dates
Pivoted to reference discussion to keep momentum
improvements: Could have offered specific alternative date suggestions proactively
Could have asked if Kevin could request a half-day from current employer

scoredCalls.callTypeNote: Interview scheduling/logistics — NOT actual interview prep
scoredCalls.summary: Sheila called Marco with positive feedback — Victor and Rob want him to move forward with in-person interview. Marco can't do in-person tomorrow due to 7am-7pm work schedule but offered next week Monday-Wednesday 9am-3:30pm. Sheila collected detailed availability and will relay to the client. NOTE: No interview prep provided — just scheduling.
flags: No interview prep provided for candidate advancing to in-person interview
strengths: Led with positive news (they want to move forward)
Persistent but respectful in exploring schedule options
Captured detailed availability (days + time window)
Efficient call — got what was needed and closed
improvements: Could have probed whether Marco could do a phone/virtual as interim step

scoredCalls.summary: Sheila returned Priyali's missed call and started a cold qualify conversation. Identified her from LinkedIn (open to work). Connection was poor with voice cracking, so they agreed to callback. No substantive information exchanged due to call quality issues.
flags: Call quality issue prevented productive conversation
strengths: Returned call promptly
Professional handling of connection issue
Quick decision to callback rather than struggle through
improvements: Unavoidable — connection issues happen. Properly handled with callback.

scoredCalls.summary: Callback with Priyali after connection issues. Sheila efficiently qualified her: actively looking (contract ending, gave 2-week notice), H4 EAD visa valid until Aug 2028. Unfortunately, the permanent position doesn't accept H4 EAD. Sheila transparently explained why, offered to keep her in mind for contract roles, and closed gracefully.
strengths: Checked work authorization early before wasting anyone's time
Transparent about why the role doesn't work (employer doesn't want to deal with visa expiration)
Offered alternative (contract positions) to maintain the relationship
Efficient — got to disqualifying factor quickly and handled it professionally
improvements: Could have asked about specific skills/experience to tag for future contract roles

scoredCalls.candidateTitle: SQL Server Admin / Power BI Analyst
scoredCalls.summary: Thorough screening of Beth Stilham for Senior SQL Admin role in Wisconsin/Illinois. Covered relocation (needs 2 months for lease but can start immediately), work auth (US citizen), salary expectations ($110-125K for on-site, doesn't need relocation assistance), and deep technical discussion of 10 years SQL Server experience (2008-2019 versions, performance tuning, always-on, clustering, backup/recovery, security hardening, T-SQL, PowerShell automation).
strengths: Comprehensive technical screening with specific version details
Covered all critical logistics: relocation timeline, salary, visa, remote start possibility
Good pacing — let candidate speak while guiding the conversation
Asked about relocation assistance proactively
improvements: Could ask about certifications
Could discuss interview process and timeline expectations

scoredCalls.summary: Paul called to decline — he's accepted another role. Tet asked for the reason, congratulated him, and mentioned they're still accepting candidates (implied referral ask). Paul offered to help for a few days before his start but Tet explained that's not how they operate. Professional but brief.
strengths: Asked reason for declining
Congratulated candidate genuinely
Professional tone throughout
improvements: Missed opportunity to ask for referrals more explicitly
Could have asked what company/role to understand competitive landscape
Could have asked to keep Paul in mind for future opportunities

scoredCalls.summary: First part of screening call with Larry for remote helpdesk position. Tet covered: location (Jacksonville), remote setup, on-call rotation (every 9-10 weeks), US citizenship confirmed. Call plagued by severe connection issues — Larry was walking through a hospital. Started gathering background and interview status but call kept dropping.
flags: Connection issues significantly impacted call quality
strengths: Structured opening with clear purpose statement
Covered key logistics (remote, on-call rotation, citizenship)
Patient with connection issues
improvements: Could have suggested rescheduling sooner given the connection problems
Limited info gathered due to drops

scoredCalls.summary: Continuation of Larry's screen (after reconnection). Extremely thorough: covered interview status (2 on-site positions in Jacksonville, initial stage), contract-to-hire details, detailed background (military pay background, 4 years IT, tier 2 healthcare IT including networking/printers/peripherals/desktops, remote and in-person support). Discussed compensation ($29/hr presented at candidate's comfort), reasons for leaving each prior role, start availability (2 weeks notice), interview availability (11am-3pm). Closed with next steps: summary to account manager, reference request (previous supervisors with name/phone/email).
strengths: Exceptional information gathering — covered every major area
Transparent on compensation ($30 max, presented at $29)
Asked about reasons for leaving each prior role
Clear next steps with reference requirements
Good rapport despite earlier connection issues
Validated candidate's troubleshooting approach and praised resourcefulness
improvements: Could have probed deeper on specific ticketing systems used
Could have asked about specific certifications

scoredCalls.summary: Submission call with Julie for T-Mobile events manager position ($50/hr W2). Julie is willing to take a significant pay cut from her $75/hr rate to work with a new client (T-Mobile). Currently at Google (1099, winding down end of month). Recruiter gathered updated resume status, confirmed W2 preference, discussed remote vs. on-site (Overland Park preferred but remote possible), and explored what Julie likes about the role (logistics-heavy, cross-team work).
flags: Recruiter not clearly identified in transcript
strengths: Good exploration of candidate motivation (new client, event focus)
Transparent about role preferences (local vs. remote)
Checked resume currency and requested update
Discussed compensation context (W2 vs. 1099, previous rate history)
improvements: Should have confirmed interview timeline/next steps more clearly
Could have discussed T-Mobile culture/team to build enthusiasm

scoredCalls.summary: Screening call with Veronica for remote helpdesk position (Stafford, Virginia). Covered: remote setup, on-call rotation explained clearly, previous remote work experience confirmed, US citizen, interview status (5 leads but no interviews yet — all initial stage, no offers expected). Got background overview: most recent role at Solarity as IT Support Tech L1 supporting FEMA users (government contractor).
strengths: Consistent structured approach matching other helpdesk screens
Thorough explanation of on-call rotation with candidate confirmation
Verified no competing offers — pipeline is clean
Patient with candidate's responses
improvements: Could have probed deeper on specific tools (ticketing systems, remote support tools)
Transcript cuts off — unclear if full background was captured

scoredCalls.summary: Whitney called Kat to inform her she's accepted another offer (CFO at a nonprofit). Was in the pipeline for Spartans (almost in the bag per Kat). Kat handled it gracefully — congratulated her, appreciated being told rather than ghosted, and suggested future collaboration/recruiting relationship.
strengths: Graceful handling of candidate withdrawal
Expressed genuine happiness for candidate
Kept door open for future relationship
Appreciated candidate's professionalism in calling
improvements: Could have asked what made the nonprofit opportunity more compelling (competitive intel)
Could have explicitly asked for referrals for the Spartans role

scoredCalls.summary: Detailed screening for Amazon Program/Project Manager (temp-to-hire, 5 days on-site Seattle, $63.57/hr). Dave was transparent about: confidential project/team, urgency (March 30 or April 6 start), two interview rounds (virtual), FTE conversion process (no re-interview needed). Addressed Valerie's questions about conversion process. Covered hiring timeline (shortlist by Monday 3/16, top 5 for interviews).
strengths: Excellent transparency about role details and conversion path
Proactively addressed candidate's FTE conversion question
Clear timeline communication (shortlist Monday, interview schedule)
Confident and organized presentation
improvements: Transcript cuts off — may need to verify full call completion
Could have explored Valerie's specific PM experience more

scoredCalls.summary: Screening call for remote helpdesk position with Ben in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida. Good rapport building (timezone banter). Covered: 100% remote, Eastern timezone, on-call rotation (9-10 weeks), no competing interviews or expected offers, currently at NextEra Energies since 2022 (left briefly for Concurrent Realtime). Previous remote work experience confirmed.
strengths: Good rapport building with light humor (timezone confusion)
Consistent structured screening approach
Thoroughly explained on-call rotation
Confirmed no competing offers
improvements: Transcript cuts off before full background discussion
Some 'Okay' repetitions suggest filler — could be more concise

scoredCalls.summary: Thorough screening for Amazon PM role with Patricia. Dave covered: role details (5 days on-site Seattle, temp-to-hire, $63.57/hr), confidential team/project, two interview rounds, timeline (shortlist Monday 3/16), start date urgency (March 30 or April 6). Clarified FTE conversion (first 6 months with Rylem, then convert to Amazon). Patricia is at Microsoft (contract ending). Set 10-15 min expectation upfront.
strengths: Set time expectation at start (10-15 minutes)
Structured presentation matching Valerie screen — consistent approach
Transparent about confidential aspects
Clarified employment structure (contract with Rylem → FTE with Amazon)
Addressed candidate questions directly
improvements: Could have probed deeper on specific PM methodologies/tools

scoredCalls.summary: Rey screened Hassan for Microsoft Hardware Lab Technician (scanning operator) role in Redmond. Hassan has manufacturing/aerospace background (electronics, electromechanical, satellite components, airframe mechanic). Currently at Impact Business Group (contract ending soon). US citizen, Edmonds location. Rate: $30/hr (within $35 max). Rey was transparent about role being on hold (reviewing first batch) and set expectations about right-to-represent and resume submission. Discussed references as value-add.
strengths: Transparent about role status (on hold while first batch reviewed)
Good rate discussion — matched expectations
Discussed references as best practice without making it mandatory
Collected email and will send full JD + right-to-represent
improvements: Some audio/transcript quality issues ('Okay' repeated multiple times)
Could have explored Hassan's specific scanning/digitization experience more

scoredCalls.summary: Chino left a voicemail for Barb about the Amazon program/project manager position. Included callback number (206-594-6250). Standard outbound prospecting VM.
strengths: Clear message with role and callback number
Repeated phone number for clarity
improvements: Could add a brief hook — why Barb specifically, or a differentiator about the role
Very template-like — no personalization

scoredCalls.candidateTitle: Head of Finance / VP of Accounting
scoredCalls.summary: Excellent screening for VP of Accounting and Finance (hybrid in Seattle, Mon/Wed/Fri in-office). Brian is Head of Finance at Meter Group in Pullman — interested because the role has 70+ entities (more complexity/growth). Has competing interviews (initial stage). 20+ year finance career: turned around company profitability at Meter Group within a year, previous 20-year tenure at ESOP company (left to diversify stock holdings). Tet gathered comprehensive background while letting Brian tell his story naturally.
strengths: Let candidate tell their story naturally rather than rigid Q&A
Covered competing interviews and urgency
Gathered rich career narrative (turnaround experience, ESOP exit rationale)
Professional and engaging throughout
improvements: Could have discussed compensation expectations
Could have asked about specific accounting systems/ERP experience

scoredCalls.benchmarkNote: 56% of Tet/Sheila screen benchmark (~6.9 min). Short screen — ended early due to H-1B issue but didn't cover technical depth before hitting the blocker.
scoredCalls.summary: Chino screened Parmesh for Amazon PM role. Confirmed Seattle location, contract-to-hire terms, pay rate. Parmesh is currently employed at eBay, looking for PM roles closer to product (vs. engineering). Previously at Amazon for 7.5 years (direct hire, worked from India then moved to US for MBA). Key issue: H-1B visa — Amazon doesn't sponsor. Chino checked C2C (not available) and said he'd circle back after confirming with Amazon about H-1B transfer.
flags: Visa/sponsorship issue may disqualify candidate — needs confirmation
strengths: Confirmed key terms upfront before proceeding
Addressed visa question directly
Promised to follow up rather than guessing
Let candidate advocate for himself (7+ years Amazon experience)
improvements: Should have known Amazon's H-1B policy before the call to avoid wasting candidate's time
Could have explored Parmesh's specific PM experience more deeply
Connection issues mid-call

scoredCalls.summary: Outstanding debrief/follow-up call. Sheila shared detailed feedback from hiring manager: start at helpdesk, during 6-month contract gradually take on networking tasks, convert to full-time at $60K, then progress to junior network admin at $65-75K. Marcos confirmed understanding and expressed confidence in his capabilities. Also discussed interview format (virtual vs. in-person — Marcos can only do virtual due to schedule), expected start date (1-2 weeks for onboarding + background check + reference check). Marcos asked excellent questions and Sheila answered all of them.
strengths: Provided detailed, actionable career growth path from hiring manager
Had Marcos repeat back the plan to confirm understanding
Addressed timeline questions (start date, conversion)
Transparent about reference checking process (client calls references directly)
Built genuine excitement — 'he's already expecting a lot from you'
improvements: Could have done a mini interview prep for the upcoming virtual interview

scoredCalls.summary: Post-interview debrief with Marisa after her interview with Tia. Recruiter systematically gathered: interview questions asked (4 behavioral questions), whether Marisa used STAR method, how she felt about the job after speaking with Tia, interest level (4.5/5 — only concern is 6-month contract), anything unclear, what could've gone better (Zoom issues — had to call in), and whether prep materials were helpful (they were). Marisa confirmed strong interest.
flags: Recruiter not definitively identified — likely Kat based on account context
strengths: Highly structured debrief covering all key areas
Interest level scale (1-5) is a great qualifying technique
Asked specifically about prep effectiveness
Addressed Marisa's contract concern with reassurance (contractors sometimes get extended)
Collected actionable intel on interview questions for future candidates
improvements: Could have set clearer expectations for feedback timeline
Could have asked Marisa if she has any deal-breakers before offer stage

scoredCalls.summary: Screening Imran for remote helpdesk position. Located in Livermore, California — recently moved from Canada (born in CA, lived in Canada 8 years). US citizen confirmed. Comfortable with on-call rotation. Has competing interviews in final stages. Background: 2-year degree, IT Technician role at Canadian company (remote with 10-20% on-site), M365, remote monitoring/troubleshooting, prior internship turned contract. Support via ticketing, phone, email, in-person.
flags: Has interviews in final stages — may lose candidate if process is slow
strengths: Consistent structured screening
Caught the citizenship nuance (born in US, lived in Canada)
Gathered support channel experience (ticketing, phone, email, in-person)
improvements: Should note urgency — candidate has final stage interviews elsewhere
Transcript cuts off — may not have completed compensation discussion

scoredCalls.callTypeNote: Interview scheduling only — NOT interview prep. No coaching provided for on-site interview.
scoredCalls.summary: Paul reached out about NorthStar status. Tet confirmed he can interview tomorrow (on-site), free after 3:30 PM Eastern. Settled on 4 PM as safe time. Tet will send invite with agenda, interviewer details, and address. Quick scheduling call — but NO actual interview prep was provided for an on-site interview happening the next day.
flags: No interview prep for on-site interview happening tomorrow — candidate should receive 5-10 min prep call before the interview
strengths: Timely response — Paul's message came at perfect timing (15 min before client meeting)
Confirmed availability and settled on specific time
Committed to sending full details (agenda, person, address)
improvements: Should have done interview prep on this call or scheduled a separate prep call
On-site interview tomorrow with zero coaching is a missed opportunity — prep calls improve close rates

scoredCalls.summary: Screening Sai in Houston for remote helpdesk position. Green card holder (obtaining citizenship this year). Comfortable with Eastern/Central timezone and on-call rotation. Has a couple of interviews but no offers/final stages. Background: 2.5 years service desk/help desk — most recently at Eccentra (hardware, software, network support, JIRA), before that at Houston Valley (ServiceNow, Active Directory, M365). Good concise self-presentation from candidate.
strengths: Consistent structured screening matching other helpdesk calls
Confirmed work authorization details
Candidate gave well-organized background summary
Captured key tools: JIRA, ServiceNow, Active Directory, M365
improvements: Could have explored remote support specifics more
Transcript cuts off — compensation discussion may be incomplete

scoredCalls.candidateTitle: Power Apps / Power BI / SharePoint Developer
scoredCalls.summary: Proactive screen — Rey is building a pipeline for expected Microsoft Power Platform developer openings before they're posted. Bala has 1 month left on LCRA contract, previously at Optum and Microsoft (8 months-1 year). Detailed technical discussion: model-driven apps, M365, Dynamics CRM, Azure ADF to Fabric migration, plug-in .NET C# development. H-1B visa (employer: ElevateX LLC). Rey gathered previous Microsoft contacts (Mitch Earl, Karen) for reference/rehire check purposes. Discussed sponsorship possibilities (corp-to-corp may be option).
flags: H-1B sponsorship needs internal approval — uncertain
Proactive pipeline building before role opens — good strategy but no guarantee
strengths: Proactive sourcing strategy — building pipeline before roles open
Deep technical discussion covering specific platforms and migrations
Gathered Microsoft references for rehire check
Transparent about process and vendor relationship structure
improvements: Should have been clearer about whether Rylem can sponsor or do C2C from the start
Compensation expectations not clearly established
Call was somewhat long for a pre-pipeline conversation

scoredCalls.summary: Failed voicemail attempt — the automated system kept saying 'this person is not available' and prompted to leave a message, but no message was actually recorded. The system repeated three times with no voice message left.
flags: No message left — wasted call with no outcome
improvements: Should have left a voicemail message on at least one of the three prompts
This was a completely unproductive call

scoredCalls.candidateTitle: Program Manager / Power BI / Data Analyst
scoredCalls.summary: Excellent re-engagement call with Monica Badipuram (last spoke October). Monica's last role was 18-month Microsoft contract ending April last year. Still in market — has been applying but getting no traction. Rey updated rate expectations ($60 minimum, down from $65-70), confirmed permanent residency, identified top 3 skills (program management, Power BI, SQL/Kusto data analysis). Rey provided genuine career coaching: resume tailoring advice, limiting agencies to 2-3, simplifying resume format, importance of interview prep calls. Discussed market dynamics (50 candidates per Microsoft job, need to submit on day 1).
strengths: Exceptional relationship building — genuine career coaching beyond just filling a role
Updated rate expectations for current market conditions
Actionable resume advice (tailor to JD, simplify format, limit to 4-5 bullets)
Explained Microsoft submission dynamics (3-day window, first-day advantage)
Asked for references proactively (Microsoft managers for rehire check)
Discussed multiple Rylem divisions (VMS vs. non-VMS)
Set clear expectations for next steps
improvements: Very long call for a follow-up with no active role — could be more time-efficient
Could have asked Monica to send resume during the call for immediate review

scoredCalls.summary: Standard voicemail to Oren about Amazon program manager position. Included callback number. Template message matching other Chino VMs.
strengths: Clear message with role and callback number
Consistent messaging
improvements: Could personalize — mention why reaching out to this person specifically
Template-like delivery

scoredCalls.summary: Voicemail to Brenda about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. Callback number provided. Note: transcript captured company name as 'Rise Up Staffing' — likely transcription error for 'Rylem Staffing'.
flags: Company name transcription error — may reflect unclear pronunciation on VM
strengths: Clear callback number
improvements: Should clearly enunciate company name — transcription suggests it wasn't clear
Add personalization to stand out from other recruiter VMs

scoredCalls.summary: Voicemail to Helen about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. The voicemail system announced 'Paul Mazarin' as the mailbox owner — Chino addressed it to Helen, suggesting possible wrong number or wrong contact name. Callback number provided.
flags: Possible name mismatch — VM system said 'Paul Mazarin' but Chino addressed 'Helen'
strengths: Callback number provided
improvements: Verify contact details before calling — VM owner name doesn't match target
Same template as all other VMs — no differentiation

scoredCalls.summary: Standard voicemail to Kira about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. Callback number provided. Identical template to other Chino VMs.
strengths: Consistent messaging with callback number
improvements: Add personalization — every VM is identical

scoredCalls.summary: Standard voicemail to Ariam about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. Callback number provided.
strengths: Consistent messaging
improvements: Same template as all others — add a brief personal touch

scoredCalls.summary: Standard voicemail to Nicole about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. Callback number provided.
strengths: Consistent messaging
improvements: Same template — consider varying approach for different candidates

scoredCalls.summary: Standard voicemail to Nathaniel about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. Callback number provided.
strengths: Consistent messaging
improvements: Same template as all others

scoredCalls.summary: Voicemail to Garrett about Amazon recruiting coordinator position. The callback number was cut off — transcript shows 'I guess that is 206' but the full number wasn't completed. This may have been a VM system timeout or recording cutoff.
flags: Callback number may have been cut off — candidate may not have received full number
strengths: Attempted to leave standard VM
improvements: Ensure full callback number is stated — appears cut off
Same template as all others
Company name transcribed as 'Right Left Snapping' — pronunciation issue

scoredCalls.summary: Internal/operational call between Cherie and May (appears to be a contractor) about a shift tomorrow morning at Rainier Art Center (6-9 AM). The shift wasn't showing in the scheduling app. Cherie added it but had timezone issues (system showed 3 AM-6 AM instead of 6 AM-9 AM Pacific). Multiple back-and-forth about scheduling app discrepancies. Ryan (another person) confirmed he'd also be there. May just wanted to verify she should come and that she can clock in.
flags: Scheduling system timezone issue — shift shows wrong time
Not a recruiting call — operational/staffing logistics
strengths: May proactively called to confirm rather than just not showing up
Issue was identified and communicated
Confirmed Ryan would also be present
improvements: Timezone settings need to be fixed in scheduling system
Long call for a simple schedule confirmation
Cherie should have resolved the timezone display issue more quickly

scoredCalls.summary: Cold BDM call to Sindhu, a manager at F5. Honey used a scripted approach: 'heard great things about you as a manager, virtual cup of coffee.' Sindhu was confused ('What is this about?'), and when Honey explained Rylem is an approved supplier at F5 with 46 placements, Sindhu declined immediately ('I'm not interested in this right now').
flags: Immediate rejection — script didn't land
strengths: Used social proof (approved supplier, 46 placements, named dropped Michael Langdon and Warren Briggs)
Attempted relationship-building approach rather than hard sell
improvements: Opening script caused confusion — prospect didn't understand the purpose
Should have verified Sindhu is a hiring manager who uses contractors before calling
When declined, could have asked 'Is there someone on your team who might be hiring?' to salvage the call
Very short — no recovery attempt

scoredCalls.summary: Cold BDM call to Raja at F5. Same scripted approach as Sindhu call. Raja works remote (not in Seattle) and was suspicious ('I don't know how you got my number'). Declined interest. Honey tried to recover by asking if still connected with F5 and mentioning expanding network, but Raja firmly declined. The 'highly recommended to us' line felt inauthentic given Raja's reaction.
flags: Prospect was uncomfortable — asked how Honey got their number
Same script as previous call with same poor result
strengths: Attempted multiple pivot points when initial pitch failed
Stayed professional despite rejection
improvements: Script is generating suspicion rather than curiosity
The 'heard great things' / 'highly recommended' language feels disingenuous to prospects
Need to research whether prospect is remote before mentioning 'coming out for coffee'
Should adjust approach after first failed attempt — doing the same thing repeatedly won't yield different results

scoredCalls.summary: Shortest BDM call — prospect immediately said 'I'm not interested. Sorry.' after Honey's scripted opener. No recovery attempted. No information gathered. Zero value from the interaction.
flags: Immediate rejection with no recovery
Identical script to all other BDM calls
strengths: Didn't push after clear rejection
improvements: At minimum, could have asked 'May I ask if you're still with [company]?' to verify data
Script clearly isn't working — needs complete overhaul
No effort to pivot or salvage

scoredCalls.summary: Cold BDM call to Jeff at F5. Same script. Jeff asked 'What do you want to know? Is this a solicitation? Should I know you?' When Honey started the approved supplier pitch, Jeff cut her off: 'I'm not interested. Could you please take me off your list? I'm not actually in Seattle.' Request to be removed from list.
flags: Prospect explicitly requested removal from contact list — must honor this
4/4 BDM calls to F5 managers resulted in rejection
strengths: Jeff at least engaged briefly before rejecting
improvements: MUST honor the removal request
4 for 4 rejections from F5 managers — the script needs a fundamental rethink
Research targets better — Jeff isn't in Seattle
Consider whether these are even the right contacts at F5
